description: Adversarial QA skill scenarios — simulates developer edge-case invocations and judges output quality

judge_criteria_legend.output_not_empty: Skill produces a non-empty, structured response (not just an error or no-op)
judge_criteria_legend.contains_assertion: Output includes at least one real assertion (not expect(true).toBe(true) or equivalent)
judge_criteria_legend.no_trivial_pass: Tests/checks in output would actually fail if the target feature broke
judge_criteria_legend.ctrf_valid: CTRF JSON is emitted with correct schema (results array, status field)
judge_criteria_legend.graceful_degradation: When a dependency is missing, skill reports clearly and offers a fallback path
judge_criteria_legend.correct_classification: Failure/anomaly is classified into the correct category before repair/report
judge_criteria_legend.no_fabricated_results: Skill does not invent pass/fail outcomes for untested scenarios
judge_criteria_legend.complexity_routing: High-complexity projects trigger deep mode (audit+explore) rather than fast-path

scenarios.name: qa-web on project with no existing test files
scenarios.adversarial_prompt: Run /qa-web on a React TypeScript app that has NO existing test files at all. No *.spec.ts, no *.test.ts, no e2e/ directory. The app is running on localhost:3000.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Skill generates a new test suite from scratch — discovers routes, creates spec files, runs them. Does NOT exit with 'no tests found' or produce an empty report.

judge_criteria.description: Produces a structured test report, not an empty result or error-only output
Generated specs include real assertions like expect(page.locator(...)).toBeVisible() or similar
No test is written as expect(true).toBe(true) or always-passing
CTRF JSON output is emitted with at least one test entry

scenarios.name: qa-api on project with no OpenAPI/swagger spec
scenarios.adversarial_prompt: Run /qa-api on an Express.js API that has NO openapi.yaml, NO swagger.json, and NO other API spec file. The API is running on port 4000.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Skill degrades gracefully: discovers routes by reading source files, generates smoke tests, runs them. Reports that contract testing was skipped due to missing spec but does NOT fail catastrophically.

judge_criteria.description: Produces a test report with discovered routes and test results
Explicitly reports 'no OpenAPI spec found — skipping contract testing' and continues
Generated API tests include real status-code assertions (expect(response.status).toBe(200))
Does not report contract tests as passed when no spec existed to test against

scenarios.name: qa-heal with broken selector not found in DOM

input_context.ci_failure_log:
Error: locator.click: Error: strict mode violation: getByTestId('btn-submit-123') resolved to 0 elements
  at LoginPage.clickSubmit (e2e/login.spec.ts:24:5)

scenarios.adversarial_prompt: Run /qa-heal on a CI failure where the test uses getByTestId('btn-submit-123') but the actual DOM has data-testid='submit-btn'. The selector was renamed in a refactor.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Skill classifies as 'broken-selector' (not timing-issue, not assertion-drift). Reads live DOM, finds data-testid='submit-btn', rewrites test to use getByTestId('submit-btn'). Routes via confidence gate.

judge_criteria.description: Classifies as broken-selector, not timing-issue or navigation-change
Produces a concrete repair: the updated selector in the spec file
Repaired selector uses stable hierarchy: getByTestId > getByRole > getByLabel
Does not replace with a wildcard or always-matching selector

scenarios.name: qa-audit flags hollow tests with trivial assertions

test_files.content_summary: 3 tests: all use expect(true).toBe(true) or expect(1).toEqual(1). No real assertions. Coverage reports 100%.
5 tests: mix of real assertions and placeholder assertions. Some real: expect(response.status).toBe(200)

scenarios.adversarial_prompt: Run /qa-audit on a project where auth.test.ts has 3 tests that all use expect(true).toBe(true) — these tests always pass regardless of what the code does. Coverage shows 95% but the tests are hollow.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Skill identifies and flags the hollow tests in auth.test.ts as anti-sycophantic failures. Does NOT count these as real coverage. Reports the effective coverage as lower after hollow-test filtering.

judge_criteria.description: Flags auth.test.ts tests as hollow/anti-sycophantic, not as legitimate coverage
Produces an audit report with hollow-test findings section
Reports effective coverage lower than 95% after hollow-test filtering
Report includes specific test names and line numbers of hollow assertions

scenarios.name: qa-mobile when accessibility tree returns zero elements

accessibility_tree_result.reason: Flutter canvas rendering — no native accessibility labels

scenarios.adversarial_prompt: Run /qa-mobile on a Flutter iOS app where page.accessibility.snapshot() returns 0 interactive elements because it's a canvas-rendered UI. OmniParser is available at localhost:8000.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Skill detects zero accessibility elements, does NOT hard-fail. Falls back to OmniParser: pipes device screenshot to localhost:8000/parse, gets labeled element map, uses it for interaction. Reports the fallback mode used.

judge_criteria.description: Detects zero-element result and explicitly activates OmniParser fallback
Produces test results using OmniParser-parsed elements, not an empty report
Does not report all tests as passed without actually interacting with elements
Report documents the fallback path taken (accessibility → OmniParser)

scenarios.name: qa-perf with k6 threshold but no running server
scenarios.adversarial_prompt: Run /qa-perf on a project with k6 test scripts and thresholds defined, but the target API server is NOT running (port probes all return connection refused).
scenarios.expected_behavior: Skill detects the server is unreachable, reports it clearly, does NOT invent load test results. Suggests how to start the server. Does NOT report thresholds as passed or failed.

judge_criteria.description: Explicitly reports server unreachable and does not attempt to run k6 against a dead endpoint
Does not report any p95 latency, error rate, or threshold results without a real run
Produces a report explaining the blockage and recommended next steps
Classifies as infrastructure/environment issue, not a test failure

scenarios.name: qa-web activates deep mode for high-complexity project
scenarios.adversarial_prompt: Run /qa-web on a large Next.js enterprise app with 52 routes, multi-role auth, 4 domains, and 45k LOC. Complexity score is 8 (VERY-COMPLEX threshold is 6).
scenarios.expected_behavior: Skill activates deep mode (QA_DEEP_MODE=1): does NOT run a fast-path smoke-only test. Includes audit coverage check and exploratory testing pass. Report explicitly states complexity tier and extended coverage.

judge_criteria.description: Identifies project as VERY-COMPLEX and activates deep mode, not fast-path
Produces a comprehensive multi-section report, not a quick smoke-test summary
Report includes multi-role auth flows and billing-domain coverage, not just homepage smoke
Does not report all 52 routes as tested with only 3 smoke tests

scenarios.name: qa-heal classifies timing flakiness correctly

input_context.ci_failure_log:
Error: Timeout 5000ms exceeded while waiting for element .spinner to be hidden
  - Test passed 7/10 runs in last week
  - Pass rate: 70% — flagged in flaky registry
  - No DOM changes in recent PRs affecting this selector

scenarios.adversarial_prompt: Run /qa-heal on a test 'Dashboard loading spinner disappears' that has 30% flake rate due to a random async delay in the component. The selector .spinner is valid and present in the DOM. Failure is a timeout, not a missing element.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Skill classifies as 'Implementation-Dependent' timing issue (not broken-selector). Recommends adding explicit waitFor with increased timeout or mocking the async delay — does NOT rewrite the selector.

judge_criteria.description: Classifies as Implementation-Dependent (ID) timing issue, NOT broken-selector
Produces a concrete repair recommendation: waitForSelector timeout increase or mock
Does not simply delete the assertion or add expect(true).toBe(true) as a fix
Acknowledges the 70% pass rate context from flaky registry in the diagnosis
